title: Elon Musk says Tesla should be spending on AI 'as fast as we can'
excerpt: Tesla's capital expenditure soared 142% year-over-year to $5.8 billion in the second quarter as Musk's AI spending spree ramped up.
content_html:
Elon Musk says Tesla should spend even more on AI — even if some money ends up being wasted.

The EV giant's capital expenditure soared 142% year-over-year to $5.8 billion in the second quarter as Musk's AI spending spree ramped up.

Speaking on an analyst call after Tesla's earnings on Thursday, Musk said that he had asked executives to keep accelerating the company's spending.

"We should be spending on capex as fast as we can spend — as fast as we can without it being too wasteful. So we're not trying to aim for some extremely high-efficiency capital spend because that would slow things down," Musk said.

Tesla is investing aggressively in new production lines and factories for its Cybercab robotaxi and Optimus humanoid robot.

The automaker recorded a negative free cash flow of $1.1 billion in the second quarter, its first shortfall since 2024, and Tesla's shares fell in premarket trading as the company's profits missed earnings expectations.

Executives told investors that AI spending will continue to grow, with Tesla's total capex spending expected to surpass $25 billion this year.

CFO Vaibhav Taneja said on the earnings call that Tesla was aiming to secure debt facilities to give it the capacity to borrow up to $30 billion.

He predicted spending would ramp up in the next 2-3 years as the company builds a new solar panel factory, installs more AI compute, and breaks ground on a massive 'Terafab' semiconductor fab that Tesla is building with SpaceX.

It comes as other tech giants burn through cash to keep up in the escalating AI race. Google recorded a negative free cash flow of nearly $6 billion in its second-quarter earnings on Wednesday and raised its capex predictions for the full year to as much as $205 billion.

Musk's comments on Tesla's spending efficiency come a year after he launched an assault on wasteful government spending with DOGE, and the world's richest man has continued to criticize government spending as prone to abuse and waste.

Musk told investors on Wednesday that Tesla's capex efficiency was "off-scale good" because the EV giant was investing in lots of productive assets like factories and infrastructure at the same time.

"I think probably this is the fastest industrial scale-up since World War II in America," Musk said.

content_html_zh:
埃隆·马斯克表示，特斯拉应该在人工智能上投入更多资金，即使有些钱最终被浪费了。

随着马斯克加大人工智能支出力度，这家电动汽车巨头第二季度的资本支出同比飙升 142% 至 58 亿美元。

周四特斯拉公布财报后，马斯克在分析师电话会议上表示，他已要求高管继续加快公司支出。

“我们应该尽可能快地支出资本支出——尽可能快地支出，但又不会太浪费。 因此，我们并不是试图以极其高效的资本支出为目标，因为这会减慢速度。”马斯克说。

特斯拉正在积极投资其 Cyber​​cab 机器人出租车和 Optimus 人形机器人的新生产线和工厂。

该汽车制造商第二季度自由现金流为负11亿美元，这是自2024年以来首次出现缺口，特斯拉股价在盘前交易中下跌，因为该公司的利润未达到盈利预期。

高管们告诉投资者，人工智能支出将继续增长，特斯拉今年的资本支出总额预计将超过 250 亿美元。

首席财务官 Vaibhav Taneja 在财报电话会议上表示，特斯拉的目标是获得债务融资，使其有能力借款至多 300 亿美元。

他预测，随着公司建造一座新的太阳能电池板工厂、安装更多人工智能计算设备，以及特斯拉与 SpaceX 合作建造的大型“Terafab”半导体工厂破土动工，未来 2-3 年内支出将增加。

与此同时，其他科技巨头也在不断烧钱以跟上不断升级的人工智能竞赛。 周三，谷歌第二季度财报显示自由现金流为负，接近 60 亿美元，并将全年资本支出预测上调至 2050 亿美元。

马斯克对特斯拉支出效率发表评论一年前，他与 DOGE 一起对浪费的政府支出发起了攻击，而这位世界首富继续批评政府支出容易被滥用和浪费。

马斯克周三告诉投资者，特斯拉的资本支出效率“超出规模”，因为这家电动汽车巨头同时投资于工厂和基础设施等大量生产性资产。

“我认为这可能是美国自二战以来最快的工业规模扩张，”马斯克说。
